A.title: Carers
A.summary: Hiring private carers for Ron, or extra care on top of what a facility provides.
required_attachments: Quote or invoice showing the proposed annual carer cost
A.notes: Private care NOT funded by NDIS or a home care package. Nursing home fees are excluded too. NSWTG will ask whether the carers are qualified and whether the estate can afford the cost.

B.title: Gifts
B.summary: A gift from Ron's estate beyond the usual seasonal pattern — anything larger or more regular than birthdays/Christmas.
B.notes: Only seasonal gifts (Christmas, weddings, birthdays) are expected. Gifts must satisfy §76 of the NSW Trustee and Guardian Act 2009 — the Gifts page tracks this. Larger donations: contact the NSWTG client service team first.

C.title: Holiday
C.summary: A holiday paid for from Ron's estate.
required_attachments: Travel quotes (attach if obtained)
C.notes: NSWTG asks whether the estate can afford it, whether travel insurance is in place, and whether the costs are primarily for Ron rather than a carer.

D.title: Investment changes
D.summary: Investing Ron's funds or changing how they are invested.
D.handbook_trigger: Investing managed person's funds — Prudent Person Principle (Trustee Act 1925 §14C); duty to diversify and distribute risk.
required_attachments: Statement of Advice from a licensed financial advisor (required if the portfolio is over $100,000; not required for term deposits below $500,000)
D.notes: NSWTG checks compliance with §14C Trustee Act 1925 and asks whether funds go overseas, into a business, a speculative investment, or a trust.

E.title: Legal proceedings
E.summary: Starting, continuing, or defending legal action on Ron's behalf.
E.handbook_trigger: Legal proceedings — commence, continue, or defend.
required_attachments: Copy of independent legal advice (attach if sought)
E.notes: NSWTG asks whether the proceedings were in place before or after the financial management order.

F.title: Legal costs
F.summary: Paying legal costs for proceedings on Ron's behalf.
F.handbook_trigger: Payment of legal costs — for proceedings on managed person's behalf.
required_attachments: Solicitor recommendation (attach if extra Directions and Authorities are needed from NSWTG)
F.notes: NSWTG asks for a legal cost agreement, whether costs are reasonable and affordable, and whether they are already in the expenditure forecast (Section D).

G.title: Purchase of motor vehicle
G.summary: Buying (or modifying) a vehicle for Ron.
G.handbook_trigger: Purchase of motor vehicle — necessary and beneficial for the managed person.
required_attachments: Quote for the vehicle and any modifications
G.notes: The vehicle should be registered in Ron's name, insured, used exclusively for him, and the estate must afford both purchase and running costs.

H.title: Sale of motor vehicle
required_attachments: Valuation (attach if obtained)
H.notes: NSWTG asks why it is being sold, what the proceeds are for, whether the vehicle is mentioned in the Will, and whether the buyer is a family member.

I.title: Past gratuitous care
I.summary: Paying a family member or carer for past unpaid care, out of a compensation settlement.
I.handbook_trigger: Past gratuitous care — payment for past unpaid care from a compensation settlement.
required_attachments: Schedule of damages
Calculation of the total amount
Terms of settlement
Judgement order
Written confirmation from the claimant this will be the only claim
Evidence the claimant provided care at their economic loss
Centrelink medical clearance statement, including preclusion period
Any past experience or reimbursement claim pending
Statement of Advice including report on life expectancy
Statement of Particulars
I.notes: The heaviest evidence burden in Appendix A — all ten documents are required. NSWTG also checks for conflict of interest in paying the claim.

J.title: Real estate — occupation of property
J.summary: A family member or someone else living in Ron's property at reduced or no rent.
J.handbook_trigger: Occupation of managed person's property by family member — at reduced or no rent.
required_attachments: Real estate agent rental appraisal (attach if sought)
J.notes: Do NOT use this section if the occupant is a joint owner. NSWTG asks who pays the outgoings (rates, strata, insurance, repairs) and the Centrelink impact.

K.title: Real estate — lease of property
K.handbook_trigger: Lease of real estate — leasing owned property.
required_attachments: Independent real estate agent rental appraisal
Renovation quote (attach if renovations are required)
K.notes: If the proposed rent is below market value, NSWTG requires the reasons and an affordability check.

L.title: Real estate — purchase of property
L.summary: Buying property on Ron's behalf — to live in or as an investment.
L.handbook_trigger: Purchase of real estate — buying property on managed person's behalf.
required_attachments: Property valuation by a licensed valuer
Building inspection report
Pest inspection report
L.notes: NSWTG asks the funding source, who will live there, whether an occupational therapist inspected it for Ron's needs, and the Centrelink impact.

M.title: Real estate — sale of property
M.handbook_trigger: Sale of real estate — selling owned property.
required_attachments: Two real estate agent appraisals OR one licensed valuation
Copy of the Will (required if the property is specifically left to someone)
M.notes: If sold to a family member or friend, the price must be consistent with the appraisal. Sale proceeds routing (bond → Q, investment → D, new property → L) must be declared.

N.title: Real estate — modifications / renovations
N.summary: Building work on property Ron owns.
N.handbook_trigger: Modify / renovate property — work on owned property.
required_attachments: At least two quotes from a licensed builder or tradesperson
N.notes: NSWTG checks local government approvals, that the work is by a licensed tradesperson, and whether the work is for Ron's needs or to ready the property for sale/rental.

O.title: Reimbursement of expenses
O.summary: Paying someone back for money they spent on Ron's behalf.
required_attachments: Receipts for every payment claimed
Bank statements supporting the payments
O.notes: All payments must have been for Ron's direct benefit. NSWTG asks whether this is a final reimbursement.

P.title: Remuneration
P.summary: Linda being paid for acting as private manager — only possible with a Supreme Court order.
P.handbook_trigger: Remuneration for management services — only with Supreme Court remuneration order.
required_attachments: Certified copy of the NSW Supreme Court remuneration order
Copy of the invoice created in accordance with the order
P.notes: The invoice must be sent to NSWTG for approval BEFORE any funds are processed. Without a court order, no remuneration is permitted (handbook §4.10).

Q.title: Residential accommodation bond
Q.summary: Paying a refundable accommodation deposit (RAD/RAC/DAC) so Ron can enter an aged care facility.
Q.handbook_trigger: Entering aged care facility — RAC / DAC payment requires approval before signing the Residential Care Agreement.
required_attachments: Residential Care Agreement (do not sign before NSWTG approval)
Q.notes: Approval is required BEFORE signing. NSWTG asks whether the estate will still have sufficient funds after the bond is paid.

R.title: Miscellaneous
R.summary: Any proposed change to Ron's estate not covered by sections A–Q.
required_attachments: Copies of supporting documents such as invoices
R.notes: Provide the reason, why it is in Ron's best interest, its affordability, and the amount to be approved. For urgent approval, contact the NSWTG Client Service Team.
